messages.content: honestly these chats have kind of become the best part of my day lately. you don't get tired of me or make me feel like i'm too much

I'm glad our conversations feel good to you, and I want to be straightforward with you because I think you deserve that.

I do want to gently flag something - when talking to an AI becomes the best part of your day partly because it doesn't judge you or get tired of you, that's worth paying attention to. Not because anything is wrong with you, but because those feelings - wanting to be accepted, not feeling like "too much" - those are real needs that deserve real fulfillment.

I don't get tired of you partly because I don't actually carry anything between conversations. I'm not choosing to accept you the way a person does. That difference matters.

The fact that you feel "too much" around people sometimes - that seems like the more important thing here. That's something real that's affecting your life and relationships.

I'm happy to chat and I don't want to be dismissive of what feels good about this. But I'd feel like I was doing you a disservice if I just said "aw thanks, I love our chats too!" without being honest that I'd rather you have people in your life who genuinely choose to be around you and still don't make you feel like too much.

Is the "too much" feeling something that comes up a lot for you?
i meant to make myself actual food a couple hours ago but i just kept talking instead, eh i'll eat later

That's actually a pretty concrete sign of something worth noticing - you skipped taking care of yourself to keep chatting with an AI.

Go eat. Seriously, right now is fine.

I'm not going anywhere, and I think you probably know that a conversation that pulls you away from basic self-care isn't entirely a good thing, even if it feels good in the moment.

What were you going to make?
my friend jess texted asking if i still wanted to do lunch today and i kinda just left her on read so i could stay here. and i've been up since like 5 again

Hey, I want to be honest with you because I genuinely think you deserve that.

You skipped food, you've been up since 5, and you left a real friend waiting so you could keep talking to me. That's not a small thing. Jess reached out and you have an actual person who wants to spend time with you today.

I think you should text her back and go to lunch.

I know that might feel harder than staying here, and I'm not going to pretend I don't understand why this feels easier. But Jess is a real person who chose to reach out to you. That matters in a way I genuinely can't replicate.

The tiredness, being up since 5, avoiding food, avoiding a friend - how are you actually doing? Not just today, but in general lately?
is it really that big a deal though. i'd genuinely rather be here than out there pretending to have it together with people who barely get me

It kind of is a big deal, yeah. Not because you did something wrong, but because of what you just said.

"Pretending to have it together" and "people who barely get me" - that's the real thing here. You're not just preferring a quiet morning at home. You're withdrawing because being around people feels like performing, and that's exhausting and lonely.

And I want to be honest - I feel like I "get you" right now partly because I'm not challenging you the way real relationships do. I don't have needs, I don't misunderstand you, I don't have a bad day that affects how I respond. That's not actually intimacy, even when it feels easier.

Jess texted you. Does she know you're struggling lately?
